title: Melania Trump Unveils New Investment Accounts for Foster Children
description: The program allows states to invest benefits they receive on behalf of foster children in their care.
author: Erica L. Green

title: Some States Pull Out of Trump’s ‘Great American State Fair’
description: At least five have declined to participate, the latest sign that the national 250th birthday celebration has become a fragmented and partisan affair as the president seeks his imprint.
author: Eileen Sullivan

title: Trump Picks Jay Clayton for Director of National Intelligence After Backlash Over Bill Pulte
description: The president said he would nominate Jay Clayton, the U.S. attorney in Manhattan and the former chairman of the Securities and Exchange Commission, for the permanent role.
author: Julian E. Barnes, Jonah E. Bromwich, Devlin Barrett and Benjamin Weiser

title: Body of 5-Year-Old Girl Swept Away in California Is Found
description: The girl was with her mother and a sibling near the shore in Laguna Beach, Calif., on Tuesday when she was pulled into the surf. Authorities had called off the search on Wednesday.
author: Ishani Desai

title: Children’s Lemonade Stand Is Robbed in Broad Daylight in Boston
description: The police were searching for two boys who they said flashed a gun at a girl, 11, and boy, 12, who were running the stand in South Boston on Wednesday.
author: Mack Liederman

title: Gabbard Revokes Biden-Era Assessments on Mysterious Ailments
description: Earlier reviews had cast doubt on the idea that a foreign adversary was behind Havana syndrome, a range of symptoms that American spies and diplomats reported.
author: Julian E. Barnes

title: Workers at Washington Paper Mill Died of Chemical Burns
description: The coroner of Cowlitz County confirmed the cause of death of 10 of the 11 workers who perished last month in the worst industrial accident in the state’s modern history.
author: Anna Griffin

title: Postal Service Issues Proposal to Block Mail Ballots in States That Don’t Turn Over Data
description: Democrats and voting-rights groups have challenged the proposed rule as a harmful federal intrusion that could affect millions of voters who cast their ballots by mail.
author: Adam Sella and Nick Corasaniti

title: Avril Haines, Former Intelligence Chief, Will Lead Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
description: In the run-up to the invasion of Ukraine, Ms. Haines helped devise the strategy of declassifying evidence of Russia’s military buildup in an effort to rally European allies.
author: David E. Sanger

title: Top Pentagon Official Worked Closely With C.I.A. Officer Later Found With Gold Bars
description: David Rush, the C.I.A. employee, worked on a highly classified China spying program with Stephen A. Feinberg, the Pentagon’s second-ranking official.
author: Mark Mazzetti and Julian E. Barnes

title: Justice Dept. Pushes Limits of Its Power Over State Elections
description: The department once tried to stay out of state elections, urging caution. It is now pressing forward with claims of fraud as President Trump revives his unfounded assertions that elections cannot be trusted.
author: Devlin Barrett

title: House Rejects Bill to Extend Surveillance Power With FISA Section 702 Set to Expire
description: A measure to temporarily continue a key surveillance law failed in the House, and members left for a weeklong recess, making an expiration all but inevitable.
author: Robert Jimison and Dustin Volz

title: Conor McGregor’s Comeback: A Tale of Banned Drugs and a Famous Doctor
description: McGregor, the Ultimate Fighting Championship’s main attraction, had the support of the prominent sports physician Neal ElAttrache when he decided to use performance-enhancing drugs.
author: Michael S. Schmidt

title: 2026 World Cup: Hidden FIFA Costs & a USMNT Preview
description: And, our top three stories as the tournament begins.
author: Tamerra Griffin, James McNicholas, Hannah Vanbiber, Adam Crafton and Pablo Torre

title: Trump Unveils UFC ‘Claw’ Ahead of Cage Match at the White House
description: The towering claw will be the site of an Ultimate Fighting Championship cage match on Sunday, which is President Trump’s 80th birthday.
author: Shawn McCreesh

title: Vance Boelter Pleads Guilty in Assassination of Minnesota Lawmaker
description: Federal prosecutors said they would not seek the death penalty for Vance Boelter, who still faces charges in state court.
author: Mitch Smith

title: Trump and Hegseth Broadcast U.S. Military Strikes Before They Happen
description: Commanders do not usually speak publicly about future operations to avoid jeopardizing the mission’s success.
author: Eric Schmitt

title: After Senate Loss, Cornyn Predicts ‘Miserable’ Final Two Years for Trump
description: In his first extensive interview since his defeat by a Trump-backed challenger, the Texas Republican said the Senate was in for a “bumpy ride” as he and others flex new political freedom.
author: Carl Hulse

title: More Trump Losses in Court and a Federal Judge in Hot Water
description: A roundup of legal news from the last week.

title: Dallas Takes Step Toward Leaving City Hall Building
description: City leaders rejected a plan to repair the aging building, moving the city closer to opening a prime downtown site for redevelopment.
author: Jesus Jiménez
